title: Creating Experiments
text: Creating experiment object requires three key components: Expression Matrix: Numeric data variables rows samples columns Sample Information: DataFrame containing sample metadata (groups, batches, etc.) Variable Information: DataFrame describing variables (proteins, peptides, glycan compositions, etc.) also need specify experiment metadata. Now let’s make glycomic experiment together.

title: Creating Experiments
text: sample information table records metadata sample. Conditions, groupings, batches… Anything describe samples can put . recommend least include group column factor conditions, used throughout glyclean, glystats, glyvis. Let’s build sample information table:
code: col_data <- DataFrame(   group = factor(c("A", "A", "A", "B", "B", "B"), levels = c("A", "B")),   batch = factor(c(1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 2), levels = c(1, 2)),   row.names = c("S1", "S2", "S3", "S4", "S5", "S6") ) col_data #> DataFrame with 6 rows and 2 columns #>       group    batch #>    <factor> <factor> #> S1        A        1 #> S2        A        2 #> S3        A        1 #> S4        B        2 #> S5        B        1 #> S6        B        2

title: Creating Experiments
text: variable information table describes measurement represents. use glyread package, variable information table automatically generated. can also provide variable information table. GlycomicSE, least following columns present: glycan_composition: Required. Glycan composition glyrepr::glycan_composition() object GlycoproteomicSE, least following columns present: protein: Required. UniProt accession (character) protein_site: Required. Glycosylation site position protein (integer) glycan_composition: Required. Glycan composition glyrepr::glycan_composition() object glycan structures available, additional glycan_structure column glyrepr::glycan_structure() object can also provided data frame. Let’s create variable information table:
code: row_data <- DataFrame(   glycan_composition = glyrepr::glycan_composition(     c(GalNAc = 1),     c(Gal = 1, GalNAc = 1),     c(Gal = 1, GalNAc = 1, GlcNAc = 1)   ),   glycan_structure = glyrepr::as_glycan_structure(c(     "GalNAc(a1-",     "Gal(b1-3)GalNAc(a1-",     "Gal(b1-3)[GlcNAc(a1-6)]GalNAc(a1-"   )),   row.names = c("V1", "V2", "V3") ) row_data #> DataFrame with 3 rows and 2 columns #>        glycan_composition       glycan_structure #>     <glyrepr_composition>    <glyrepr_structure> #> V1              GalNAc(1)             GalNAc(a1- #> V2        Gal(1)GalNAc(1)    Gal(b1-3)GalNAc(a1- #> V3 Gal(1)GlcNAc(1)GalNA.. Gal(b1-3)[GlcNAc(a1-..

title: Creating Experiments
text: expression matrix contains actual measurements. Layout: variables rows, samples columns. transformation (like log-transformation) needed - glycoverse functions handle internally required.

title: Creating Experiments
text: Row names must match row names variable information table, column names must match row names sample information table, order. Let’s create expression matrix:
code: # Create a simple matrix with 3 variables and 6 samples expr_mat <- matrix(   rnorm(18, mean = 10, sd = 2), # Some realistic-looking data   nrow = 3,    ncol = 6 ) rownames(expr_mat) <- rownames(row_data) colnames(expr_mat) <- rownames(col_data) expr_mat #>           S1        S2       S3        S4        S5       S6 #> V1  7.199913  9.988857 6.356365  9.434589 14.130050 6.273977 #> V2 10.510634 11.243105 9.505349  8.892601  6.738021 8.955975 #> V3  5.125473 12.296823 9.511601 11.257964 11.024854 9.894796

title: Creating Experiments
text: Now assemble experiment object: Note also provided list metadata. glycan_type field required, can provide others serve old need.
code: exp <- GlycomicSE(expr_mat, colData = col_data, rowData = row_data, metadata = list(glycan_type = "N")) exp #>  #> ── GlycomicSE ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Abundance assay: 6 samples, 3 variables #> ℹ Glycan type: N #> ℹ Row data fields: glycan_composition <comp>, glycan_structure <struct> #> ℹ Column data fields: group <fct>, batch <fct> #> ℹ Metadata fields: glycan_type <chr>

title: dplyr-Style Functions
text: Every dplyr-style function glyexp comes two variants: _col() functions: Work column data (observation metadata) SummarizedExperiment objects _row() functions: Work row data (variable metadata) SummarizedExperiment objects variants automatically update expression matrix maintain synchronization. mental model : “want update row data, use xxx_row() functions like regular dplyr functions, expression matrix update .” Filtering common operation. Say want focus group “” samples: simple example, clear benefits using glyexp way. true benefits come chaining, one operations : Now merits glyexp clear: can use dplyr-style data manipulation directly SummarizedExperiment objects, matter long operation pipeline .
code: # the SummarizedExperiment way filtered_exp1 <- toy_exp[, toy_exp$group == "A"]  # the glyexp way filtered_exp2 <- filter_col(toy_exp, group == "A")  # same result identical(filtered_exp1, filtered_exp2) #> [1] TRUE # the SummarizedExperiment way filtered_exp3 <- toy_exp colData(filtered_exp3)$batch <- factor(colData(filtered_exp3)$batch) colData(filtered_exp3)$group <- factor(colData(filtered_exp3)$group) filtered_exp3 <- filtered_exp3[   rowData(filtered_exp3)$glycan_composition %in% c("H5N2", "N3N2"),   filtered_exp3$group == "A" ]  # the glyexp way filtered_exp4 <- toy_exp |>   mutate_col(group = factor(group), batch = factor(batch)) |>   filter_col(group == "A", .drop_levels = FALSE) |>   filter_row(glycan_composition %in% c("H5N2", "N3N2"))  # the same result identical(filtered_exp3, filtered_exp4) #> [1] TRUE

title: dplyr-Style Functions
text: SummarizedExperiment, rowData colData DataFrame objects. DataFrames can row names, can accessed rownames(). row names match row column names assay (expression matrix): fact, can get names directly calling rownames() colnames() SummarizedExperiment object: dplyr functions supports row names operations, designed used tibbles, row names. glyexp, however, provide unique techqnue manipulate row names dplyr-style functions: virtual index columns. row names row column data can referred plain .variable .sample column, respectively. example, can update row column names SummarizedExperiment : understand better, can regard row column data : use dplyr-style functions.
code: rownames(colData(toy_exp)) #> [1] "S1" "S2" "S3" "S4" "S5" "S6" rownames(rowData(toy_exp)) #> [1] "V1" "V2" "V3" "V4" rownames(assay(toy_exp)) #> [1] "V1" "V2" "V3" "V4" colnames(assay(toy_exp)) #> [1] "S1" "S2" "S3" "S4" "S5" "S6" rownames(toy_exp) #> [1] "V1" "V2" "V3" "V4" colnames(toy_exp) #> [1] "S1" "S2" "S3" "S4" "S5" "S6" toy_exp |>   mutate_col(.sample = str_replace(.sample, "S", "Sample_")) |>   mutate_row(.variable = paste(protein, peptide, glycan_composition, sep = "-")) #> class: SummarizedExperiment  #> dim: 4 6  #> metadata(0): #> assays(1): data #> rownames(4): PRO1-PEP1-H5N2 PRO2-PEP2-H5N2 PRO3-PEP3-H3N2 #>   PRO3-PEP4-H3N2 #> rowData names(3): protein peptide glycan_composition #> colnames(6): Sample_1 Sample_2 ... Sample_5 Sample_6 #> colData names(2): group batch rownames_to_column(as.data.frame(rowData(toy_exp)), ".variable") #>   .variable protein peptide glycan_composition #> 1        V1    PRO1    PEP1               H5N2 #> 2        V2    PRO2    PEP2               H5N2 #> 3        V3    PRO3    PEP3               H3N2 #> 4        V4    PRO3    PEP4               H3N2 rownames_to_column(as.data.frame(colData(toy_exp)), ".sample") #>   .sample group batch #> 1      S1     A     1 #> 2      S2     A     2 #> 3      S3     A     1 #> 4      S4     B     2 #> 5      S5     B     1 #> 6      S6     B     2

title: dplyr-Style Functions
text: glyexp provides dplyr-style equivalents common data manipulation functions. function comes _col() _row() variants, automatically maintain matrix synchronization.

title: dplyr-Style Functions
text: Use dplyr-style helpers like starts_with(), ends_with(), contains():
code: # Select specific columns from sample info selected_exp <- select_col(toy_exp, group) colData(selected_exp) #> DataFrame with 6 rows and 1 column #>          group #>    <character> #> S1           A #> S2           A #> S3           A #> S4           B #> S5           B #> S6           B # Select columns from variable info (notice the index protection!) var_selected_exp <- select_row(toy_exp, glycan_composition) rowData(var_selected_exp) #> DataFrame with 4 rows and 1 column #>    glycan_composition #>           <character> #> V1               H5N2 #> V2               H5N2 #> V3               H3N2 #> V4               H3N2 # Select columns starting with "glycan" helper_exp <- select_row(toy_exp, starts_with("glycan")) rowData(helper_exp) #> DataFrame with 4 rows and 1 column #>    glycan_composition #>           <character> #> V1               H5N2 #> V2               H5N2 #> V3               H3N2 #> V4               H3N2

title: dplyr-Style Functions
text: Check expression matrix columns rearranged match:
code: # Arrange samples by batch and group arranged_exp <- arrange_col(toy_exp, batch, group) rowData(arranged_exp) #> DataFrame with 4 rows and 3 columns #>        protein     peptide glycan_composition #>    <character> <character>        <character> #> V1        PRO1        PEP1               H5N2 #> V2        PRO2        PEP2               H5N2 #> V3        PRO3        PEP3               H3N2 #> V4        PRO3        PEP4               H3N2 # Expression matrix columns follow the new sample order assay(arranged_exp) #>    S1 S3 S5 S2 S4 S6 #> V1  1  9 17  5 13 21 #> V2  2 10 18  6 14 22 #> V3  3 11 19  7 15 23 #> V4  4 12 20  8 16 24

title: dplyr-Style Functions
code: # Add a new calculated column to sample info mutated_exp <- mutate_col(   toy_exp,   group_batch = paste(group, batch, sep = "_") ) colData(mutated_exp) #> DataFrame with 6 rows and 3 columns #>          group     batch group_batch #>    <character> <numeric> <character> #> S1           A         1         A_1 #> S2           A         2         A_2 #> S3           A         1         A_1 #> S4           B         2         B_2 #> S5           B         1         B_1 #> S6           B         2         B_2 # Extract number of Hex and HexNAc complex_exp <- mutate_row(   toy_exp,   nH = as.integer(str_extract(glycan_composition, "H(\\d+)", 1)),   nN = as.integer(str_extract(glycan_composition, "N(\\d+)", 1)), ) rowData(complex_exp) #> DataFrame with 4 rows and 5 columns #>        protein     peptide glycan_composition        nH        nN #>    <character> <character>        <character> <integer> <integer> #> V1        PRO1        PEP1               H5N2         5         2 #> V2        PRO2        PEP2               H5N2         5         2 #> V3        PRO3        PEP3               H3N2         3         2 #> V4        PRO3        PEP4               H3N2         3         2

title: dplyr-Style Functions
code: # Take the first 2 samples head_exp <- slice_head_col(toy_exp, n = 2) colData(head_exp) #> DataFrame with 2 rows and 2 columns #>          group     batch #>    <character> <numeric> #> S1           A         1 #> S2           A         2 # Expression matrix automatically adjusts assay(head_exp) #>    S1 S2 #> V1  1  5 #> V2  2  6 #> V3  3  7 #> V4  4  8 # Sample randomly from variables set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility random_exp <- slice_sample_row(toy_exp, n = 3) rowData(random_exp) #> DataFrame with 3 rows and 3 columns #>        protein     peptide glycan_composition #>    <character> <character>        <character> #> V3        PRO3        PEP3               H3N2 #> V4        PRO3        PEP4               H3N2 #> V1        PRO1        PEP1               H5N2

title: dplyr-Style Functions
text: index column “sample” remains protected, everything else can renamed freely.
code: # Rename columns in sample info renamed_exp <- rename_col(toy_exp, experimental_group = group) colData(renamed_exp) #> DataFrame with 6 rows and 2 columns #>    experimental_group     batch #>           <character> <numeric> #> S1                  A         1 #> S2                  A         2 #> S3                  A         1 #> S4                  B         2 #> S5                  B         1 #> S6                  B         2

title: dplyr-Style Functions
text: functions can useful additional information stored separate tibble, want add data container. might noticed don’t alternatives dplyr::right_join() dplyr::full_join(). design joining functions glyexp used add new information data container. However, right_join() full_join() add observations resulting tibbles, suitable SummarizedExperiment objects. reason, relationship parameter fixed “many--one” joining functions glyexp. probably don’t need know , , check documentation dplyr::left_join() details.
code: # Join sample info with variable info more_sample_info <- tibble::tibble(   .sample = c("S2", "S3", "S4", "S5", "S6"),   age = c(21, 22, 23, 24, 25),   gender = c("F", "M", "F", "M", "F") ) joined_exp <- left_join_col(toy_exp, more_sample_info, by = ".sample") colData(joined_exp) #> DataFrame with 6 rows and 4 columns #>          group     batch       age      gender #>    <character> <numeric> <numeric> <character> #> S1           A         1        NA          NA #> S2           A         2        21           F #> S3           A         1        22           M #> S4           B         2        23           F #> S5           B         1        24           M #> S6           B         2        25           F

title: dplyr-Style Functions
text: Sometimes need functionality beyond glyexp’s dplyr-style functions provide. Extract component use function want.

what: Why Doesn’t glyexp Implement All dplyr Functions?
title: dplyr-Style Functions
text: glyexp implements functions preserve synchronized multi-table structure SummarizedExperiment objects. Functions like count(), distinct(), summarise(), pull() return aggregated results break original data relationships. operations, extract relevant component use standard dplyr functions:
code: # For complex aggregations toy_exp |>   colData() |>   as_tibble() |>   count(group) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   group     n #>   <chr> <int> #> 1 A         3 #> 2 B         3 # For distinct values toy_exp |>   rowData() |>   as_tibble() |>   distinct(protein) |>   pull(protein) #> [1] "PRO1" "PRO2" "PRO3" # Or just unique(rowData(toy_exp)$protein) #> [1] "PRO1" "PRO2" "PRO3"

title: dplyr-Style Functions
text: glyexp’s dplyr-style functions designed fast, safe, consistent. large datasets, consider: Filtering early pipeline reduce data size Using select_col() select_row() keep needed columns Chaining operations efficiently minimize intermediate copies
code: # Efficient pipeline: filter first, then manipulate efficient_pipeline <- toy_exp |>   filter_col(group == "A") |>          # Reduce samples early   filter_row(protein == "PRO1") |>     # Reduce variables early   select_col(group) |>                 # Keep only needed sample columns   select_row(glycan_composition)       # Keep only needed variable columns

title: dplyr-Style Functions
text: glyexp’s dplyr-style functions embody simple philosophy: “Think metadata, let data follow.” design means: Mental Model Alignment: Think terms samples variables, matrix indices Error Prevention: Automatic synchronization prevents common data analysis mistakes Familiar Syntax: know dplyr, already know glyexp Composability: Functions chain together naturally complex analyses

title: Create a GlycomicSE object — GlycomicSE
text: GlycomicSE() creates single-assay SummarizedExperiment::SummarizedExperiment() subclass glycomics data. thin wrapper around SummarizedExperiment() additional Glycoverse validation: Exactly one assay allowed. Extra assays rejected avoid ambiguous glycomics measurements. assay must contain non-negative values. Raw glycomics abundance data non-negative; log transformation handled downstream Glycoverse packages. rowData must contain glycan_composition column, column must glyrepr::glycan_composition() vector. glycan_structure column optional, present must glyrepr::glycan_structure() vector. metadata must contain glycan_type field. container experimental recognized Glycoverse packages yet. intended become recommended entry point package contracts migrate toward SummarizedExperiment-based containers.

text: columns required compulsorily variable information tibble valid experiment. depends experiment type. "glycomics": glycan_composition. "glycoproteomics": protein, protein_site, glycan_composition. "traitomics": required columns. "traitproteomics": protein, protein_site. "others": required columns. See "Column conventions" section detailed description columns. last two types experiments created glydet package. Normally need manually create .

title: Create a new experiment — experiment
text: glycoverse conserved column names sample_info var_info make everything work seamlessly. mandatory, following conventions make life easier. sample_info: group: factor, treatment/condition/grouping, used many glystats glyvis functions. batch: factor, batch information, used glyclean::correct_batch_effect(). bio_rep: factor, biological replicate, may used future. var_info: protein: character, protein Uniprot accession. protein_site: integer, glycosylation site position protein. gene: character, gene symbol. peptide: character, peptide sequence. peptide_site: integer, glycosylation site position peptide. glycan_composition: glyrepr::glycan_composition(), glycan composition. glycan_structure: glyrepr::glycan_structure(), glycan structure.

title: Create a new experiment — experiment
text: meta data can added meta_data attribute. meta_data list additional information experiment. Two meta data fields required: exp_type: "glycomics", "glycoproteomics", "traitomics", "traitproteomics", "others" glycan_type: "N", "O", "O-GalNAc", "O-Man", "O-Fuc", "O-GlcNAc", "O-Glc", "HMO", "GSL", "GAG", "GPI", NULL meta data added glycoverse packages purposes.

title: Create a new experiment — experiment
text: index columns backbone keep data synchronized: "sample" column sample_info must match column names expr_mat. "variable" column var_info must match row names expr_mat. columns act unique identifiers, ensuring expression matrix, sample information, variable information always stay sync, matter filter, arrange, subset data.

title: Create a new experiment — experiment
code: # The minimum required input is an expression matrix. expr_mat <- matrix(runif(9), nrow = 3, ncol = 3) colnames(expr_mat) <- c("S1", "S2", "S3") rownames(expr_mat) <- c("V1", "V2", "V3") experiment(expr_mat) #> Warning: `experiment()` was deprecated in glyexp 0.16.0. #> ℹ Use `GlycomicSE()` or `GlycoproteomicSE()` as the default data container. #> Warning: `print.glyexp_experiment()` was deprecated in glyexp 0.16.0. #> ℹ Use `GlycomicSE()` or `GlycoproteomicSE()` as the default data container. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the pkgdown package. #>   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/r-lib/pkgdown/issues>. #>  #> ── Others Experiment ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Expression matrix: 3 samples, 3 variables #> ℹ Sample information fields: none #> ℹ Variable information fields: none  # Or with more detailed information. sample_info <- tibble::tibble(sample = c("S1", "S2", "S3"), group = c("A", "B", "A")) var_info <- tibble::tibble(   variable = c("V1", "V2", "V3"),   glycan_composition = glyrepr::glycan_composition(c(Hex = 1)) ) experiment(expr_mat, sample_info, var_info, exp_type = "glycomics", glycan_type = "N") #> Column group converted to <factor>. #>  #> ── Glycomics Experiment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Expression matrix: 3 samples, 3 variables #> ℹ Sample information fields: group <fct> #> ℹ Variable information fields: glycan_composition <comp>

title: Real glycoproteomics experiment — real_experiment
text: real glycoproteomics experiment object. dataset derived unpublished ProteomeXchange dataset PXD063749. contains serum N-glycoproteome profiles 12 patients different liver conditions: healthy (H), hepatitis (M), cirrhosis (Y), hepatocellular carcinoma (C). Glycopeptide identification annotation performed using pGlyco3 (https://github.com/pFindStudio/pGlyco3), quantification carried pGlycoQuant (https://github.com/Power-Quant/pGlycoQuant). raw data imported glyread::read_pglyco3_pglycoquant().

title: Rename columns in the sample or variable information tibble — rename_col
text: two functions provide way rename columns sample variable information experiment() SummarizedExperiment. syntax dplyr::rename() used. example, rename "group" column sample information tibble "condition", use rename_col(exp, condition = group). Note rename "sample" column sample information tibble, well "variable" column variable information tibble. two columns used link sample variable information tibble expression matrix.

code: rename_col(exp, ...)  rename_row(exp, ...)

title: Rename columns in the sample or variable information tibble — rename_col
text: object class exp.

title: Rename columns in the sample or variable information tibble — rename_col
text: experiment() object, sample physical column sample_info, variable physical column var_info. SummarizedExperiment, sample variable identifiers live colnames(exp) rownames(exp), rather SummarizedExperiment::colData() SummarizedExperiment::rowData(). Observation verbs expose colnames(exp) virtual .sample column, variable verbs expose rownames(exp) virtual .variable column. dot-prefixed names distinguish dimension identifiers regular metadata columns. operation, virtual column removed values written back corresponding dimension names. Consequently, sample colData(exp) variable rowData(exp) remain ordinary metadata columns. names .sample .variable reserved; input containing either name corresponding metadata raises error rather overwriting column.

what: Standardize variable IDs in an experiment — standardize_variable
title: Standardize variable IDs in an experiment — standardize_variable
text: Converts meaningless variable IDs (like "GP1", "V1") meaningful, human-readable IDs based experiment type available columns. format new IDs depends exp_type format specified: glycomics: {glycan_composition}, e.g., "Hex(5)HexNAc(2)" glycoproteomics: {protein}-{protein_site}-{glycan_composition} traitomics: {motif} {trait} depending column present traitproteomics: {protein}-{protein_site}-{motif} {protein}-{protein_site}-{trait} duplicate IDs generated (e.g., composition multiple PSMs), unique integer suffix appended using unique_suffix pattern.

title: Standardize variable IDs in an experiment — standardize_variable
code: standardize_variable(exp, format = NULL, unique_suffix = "-{N}")

title: Standardize variable IDs in an experiment — standardize_variable
text: exp experiment(), GlycomicSE(), GlycoproteomicSE(). format format string specifying construct variable IDs. Use {column_name} insert values var_info columns. example, "{gene}-{glycan_composition}" produce "GENE1-Hex(5)". NULL (default), sensible format chosen based exp_type. unique_suffix string pattern making IDs unique duplicates exist. Must contain {N} replaced numeric suffix (1, 2, 3...). Default "-{N}" produces IDs like "Hex(5)-1", "Hex(5)-2".

title: Standardize variable IDs in an experiment — standardize_variable
text: exp standardized variable IDs. input container class preserved.

title: Standardize variable IDs in an experiment — standardize_variable
code: # Glycomics example expr_mat <- matrix(1:4, nrow = 2) rownames(expr_mat) <- c("V1", "V2") colnames(expr_mat) <- c("S1", "S2") sample_info <- tibble::tibble(sample = c("S1", "S2")) var_info <- tibble::tibble(   variable = c("V1", "V2"),   glycan_composition = glyrepr::glycan_composition(c(Hex = 5, HexNAc = 2)) ) exp <- experiment(expr_mat, sample_info, var_info,   exp_type = "glycomics", glycan_type = "N" ) standardize_variable(exp) #>  #> ── Glycomics Experiment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Expression matrix: 2 samples, 2 variables #> ℹ Sample information fields: none #> ℹ Variable information fields: glycan_composition <comp>  # Glycoproteomics example expr_mat <- matrix(1:4, nrow = 2) rownames(expr_mat) <- c("GP1", "GP2") colnames(expr_mat) <- c("S1", "S2") sample_info <- tibble::tibble(sample = c("S1", "S2")) var_info <- tibble::tibble(   variable = c("GP1", "GP2"),   protein = c("P12345", "P12345"),   protein_site = c(32L, 45L),   glycan_composition = glyrepr::glycan_composition(c(Hex = 5, HexNAc = 2)) ) exp <- experiment(expr_mat, sample_info, var_info,   exp_type = "glycoproteomics", glycan_type = "N" ) standardize_variable(exp) #>  #> ── Glycoproteomics Experiment ────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Expression matrix: 2 samples, 2 variables #> ℹ Sample information fields: none #> ℹ Variable information fields: protein <chr>, protein_site <int>, glycan_composition <comp>  # Custom format example standardize_variable(exp, format = "{protein}-{glycan_composition}") #>  #> ── Glycoproteomics Experiment ────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Expression matrix: 2 samples, 2 variables #> ℹ Sample information fields: none #> ℹ Variable information fields: protein <chr>, protein_site <int>, glycan_composition <comp>

title: Identification overview — summarize_experiment
text: tibble columns item n summarizing results. items include: total_composition: number glycan compositions. total_structure: number glycan structures. total_peptide: number peptides. total_glycopeptide: number unique combinations peptides, sites, glycans. total_glycoform: number unique combinations proteins, sites, glycans. total_protein: number proteins. total_glycosite: number unique combinations proteins sites. addition, _per_sample items calculated average number detected items per sample. example, composition_per_sample average number glycan compositions detected per sample.
